stem: In the expansion of \( ([[p]]x + [[q]])^{[[n]]} \), what is the coefficient of \( x^{[[k]]} \)?
solution: The term containing \( x^{k} \) in \( (px + q)^{n} \) is \( \binom{n}{k}(px)^{k} q^{n-k} \), so the coefficient of \( x^{[[k]]} \) is \( \binom{[[n]]}{[[k]]} ([[p]])^{[[k]]} ([[q]])^{[[n]] - [[k]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is only \( \binom{n}{k} \); it omits the factors \( p^{k} \) and \( q^{n-k} \).
This swaps the exponents on \( p \) and \( q \).
This omits the binomial coefficient \( \binom{n}{k} \).
This is off by one from the correct coefficient.
This is off by one from the correct coefficient.
This is off by two from the correct coefficient.

stem: In the expansion of \( \left(x^{2} + \dfrac{[[c]]}{x}\right)^{[[n]]} \), what is the coefficient of \( x^{[[m]]} \)?
solution: The general term is \( \binom{[[n]]}{j} (x^{2})^{[[n]]-j} \left(\dfrac{[[c]]}{x}\right)^{j} = \binom{[[n]]}{j} ([[c]])^{j} x^{2\cdot[[n]]-3j} \). Setting \( 2([[n]]) - 3j = [[m]] \) gives \( j = [[j]] \), so the coefficient is \( \binom{[[n]]}{[[j]]} ([[c]])^{[[j]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This omits the factor \( c^{j} \) from the term.
This omits the binomial coefficient \( \binom{n}{j} \).
This uses the wrong exponent on \( c \), swapping \( j \) and \( n-j \).
This is off by one from the correct coefficient.
This is off by one from the correct coefficient.
This is off by two from the correct coefficient.

stem: What is the coefficient of the \( [[t]] \)-th term (counting the first term as \( x^{[[n]]} \)) in the expansion of \( (x + [[c]])^{[[n]]} \)?
solution: The \( [[t]] \)-th term corresponds to index \( j = [[t]] - 1 \) (since the first term has \( j = 0 \)). Its coefficient is \( \binom{[[n]]}{[[t]]-1} ([[c]])^{[[t]]-1} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets the off-by-one shift between term NUMBER and binomial index, using \( j = t \) instead of \( j = t-1 \).
This omits the factor \( c^{t-1} \) from the term.
This omits the binomial coefficient \( \binom{n}{t-1} \).
This is off by one from the correct coefficient.
This is off by one from the correct coefficient.
This is off by two from the correct coefficient.

stem: What is the coefficient of the \( [[s]] \)-th term counted from the END of the expansion of \( ([[p]]x + [[q]])^{[[n]]} \)?
solution: The last term ( \( s=1 \) ) corresponds to index \( j = n \), so counting \( [[s]] \) terms from the end gives \( j = [[n]] - [[s]] + 1 = [[j]] \). That term's coefficient is \( \binom{[[n]]}{[[j]]} ([[p]])^{[[j]]} ([[q]])^{[[n]]-[[j]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This counts from the beginning ( \( j = s-1 \) ) instead of from the end.
This omits the factors \( p^{j} q^{n-j} \), leaving only the binomial coefficient.
This omits the binomial coefficient \( \binom{n}{j} \).
This is off by one from the correct coefficient.
This is off by one from the correct coefficient.
This is off by two from the correct coefficient.
